_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to clearly articulate intelligence requirements into well-formulated research questions and requests for information.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-A-001 (AI, cosine: 0.493), AI-T-018 (AI, cosine: 0.454).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.8/10 (raw 0.379). Shared vocabulary: [ability]. DCWF-only terms [articulate, clearly, formulated, information, intelligence, questions] vs WIDAI-only terms [analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing, business] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.876, R=0.862). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Ability to clearly articulate intelligence requirements into well-formulated res" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.515 > STS 0.379 (gap +0.137). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.8%. Shared tokens: [ability]. DCWF-only: [articulate, clearly, formulated, information, intelligence, questions]. WIDAI-only: [analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing, business].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.137 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
